description: §51.0.M <onTimeout>: the initial state .Green arms a 1000ms timer to .Yellow at engine boot. A single {"advance-time":1000} verb advances the VIRTUAL clock past the deadline, firing the timer through the rule= contract; the auto-derived @light cell + its interpolation update to Yellow. Proves timer surfaces are conformance-testable AND that the timer fires ONLY on advance-time (see the sibling on-timeout-no-advance case, which omits the verb and stays Green).
rationale: §51.0.M onTimeout: a state-child <onTimeout after=DURATION to=.Variant/> arms a timer on state entry (here at module-init for the initial state) that fires a rule=-honoring transition on expiry. Under the conformance virtual clock, that setTimeout is deterministic — it fires exactly when the case advances time past the 1000ms deadline, never on a wall clock.
